Module me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.core
Package me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx

Class FlixelSprite

java.lang.Object
me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelBasic
me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelObject
me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelSprite
All Implemented Interfaces:
com.badlogic.gdx.utils.Disposable, com.badlogic.gdx.utils.Pool.Poolable, FlixelDebugDrawable, FlixelDestroyable, FlixelDrawable, FlixelUpdatable
Direct Known Subclasses:
FlixelBar, FlixelSpriteGroup, FlixelText
public class FlixelSprite extends FlixelObject
The core building block of all Flixel games. Extends FlixelObject with graphical capabilities including texture rendering, scaling, rotation, tinting, and flipping.

Frame-based clips, Sparrow/XML atlases, and playback use a FlixelAnimationController that is not allocated by default (saves memory for large sprite counts on the order of thousands of extra sprites before overhead dominates). Call ensureAnimation() or setAnimation(FlixelAnimationController) when you need clips, then use sprite.ensureAnimation().playAnimation(...), loadSparrowFrames(...), etc.

It is common to extend FlixelSprite for your own game's needs; for example, a SpaceShip class may extend FlixelSprite but add additional game-specific fields.

      public FlixelSprite loadGraphic(com.badlogic.gdx.files.FileHandle path)
      Load's a texture and automatically resizes the size of this sprite.
      Parameters:
      path - The directory of the .png to load onto this sprite.
      Returns:
      this sprite for chaining.

      frameWidth - How wide the sprite should be.
      frameHeight - How tall the sprite should be.
      Returns:
      this sprite for chaining.

    • loadGraphic

      public FlixelSprite loadGraphic(com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Texture texture, int frameWidth, int frameHeight)
      Loads a texture and automatically resizes the size of this sprite.
      Parameters:
      texture - The texture to load onto this sprite (owned by caller).
      frameWidth - How wide the sprite should be.
      frameHeight - How tall the sprite should be.
      Returns:
      this sprite for chaining.

    • loadGraphic

      public FlixelSprite loadGraphic(String assetKey)
      Loads a cached graphic by key. The texture can be preloaded via FlixelGraphic.queueLoad() and Flixel.assets.update() in a loading state.

      This method falls back to a synchronous load if the texture is not loaded yet. Preloading is still strongly recommended to avoid mid-frame stalls.

      Parameters:
      assetKey - The key of the graphic to load.
      Returns:
      this sprite for chaining.

    • loadGraphic

      public FlixelSprite loadGraphic(String assetKey, int frameWidth)
      Loads a cached graphic by key. The texture can be preloaded via FlixelGraphic.queueLoad() and Flixel.assets.update() in a loading state.

      This method falls back to a synchronous load if the texture is not loaded yet. Preloading is still strongly recommended to avoid mid-frame stalls.

      Parameters:
      assetKey - The key of the graphic to load.
      frameWidth - The width of the graphic.
      Returns:
      this sprite for chaining.

    • loadGraphic

      public FlixelSprite loadGraphic(String assetKey, int frameWidth, int frameHeight)
      Loads a cached graphic by key. The texture can be preloaded via FlixelGraphic.queueLoad() and Flixel.assets.update() in a loading state.

      This method falls back to a synchronous load if the texture is not loaded yet. Preloading is still strongly recommended to avoid mid-frame stalls.

      Parameters:
      assetKey - The key of the graphic to load.
      frameWidth - The width of the graphic.
      frameHeight - The height of the graphic.
      Returns:
      this sprite for chaining.

    • loadGraphic

      public FlixelSprite loadGraphic(FlixelGraphic g, int frameWidth, int frameHeight)
      Loads a graphic from a FlixelGraphic.
      Parameters:
      g - The FlixelGraphic to load.
      frameWidth - The width of the graphic.
      frameHeight - The height of the graphic.
      Returns:
      this sprite for chaining.

      public FlixelSprite makeGraphic(int width, int height, com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color color)
      Creates a solid color rectangular texture on the fly.
      Parameters:
      width - The width of the graphic.
      height - The height of the graphic.
      color - The color of the graphic.
      Returns:
      this sprite for chaining.

    • applySparrowAtlas

      public void applySparrowAtlas(@NotNull @NotNull FlixelGraphic newGraphic, @NotNull @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.utils.Array<FlixelFrame> parsedFrames)
      Installs a retained FlixelGraphic and parsed Sparrow atlas frames. Called by FlixelAnimationController.loadSparrowFrames(String, com.badlogic.gdx.utils.XmlReader.Element) only; not a general API for game code.
      Parameters:
      newGraphic - Graphic wrapper already retain()ed by the caller.
      parsedFrames - Frames built from the XML (may be empty).

    • draw

      public void draw(com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.g2d.Batch batch)
      Description copied from class: FlixelBasic
      Override this function to control how the object is drawn. Doing so is rarely necessary but can be very useful.
      Specified by:
      draw in interface FlixelDrawable
      Overrides:
      draw in class FlixelBasic
      Parameters:
      batch - The batch used for rendering.

    • setGraphicSize

      public FlixelSprite setGraphicSize(int width, int height)
      Sets how large the graphic is drawn on screen (in pixels), without changing which part of the texture is used.

      This adjusts setScale(float, float) so the full current frame/region maps to the given size. It does not change TextureRegion bounds: TextureRegion#setRegionWidth/setRegionHeight only resize the source rectangle inside the texture (UVs), which crops or re-samples texels; the drawable size in this class comes from FlixelObject.getWidth()/FlixelObject.getHeight() and scale in draw(com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.g2d.Batch).

      Parameters:
      width - The drawn width in pixels (must be > 0).
      height - The drawn height in pixels (must be > 0).
      Returns:
      this sprite for chaining.

    • updateHitbox

      public FlixelSprite updateHitbox()
      Sets the hitbox to match the on-screen graphic.

      For textures drawn via currentRegion, draw(com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.g2d.Batch) uses getWidth() * |scaleX| (and height), so this folds scale into FlixelObject.setSize(float, float) and resets scale to 1 to avoid double-scaling. Sparrow/atlas frames (currentFrame) keep scale because draw(com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.g2d.Batch) sizes that path from the frame region * scale, while hitbox dimensions are still set to the same effective pixel size for Flixel.overlap(me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelBasic, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelBasic, java.util.function.BiConsumer<me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelObject, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelObject>, java.util.function.BiFunction<me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelObject, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelObject, java.lang.Boolean>).

    • updateHitbox

      public FlixelSprite updateHitbox(float width, float height)
      Updates the hitbox of this sprite to the size of the given width and height.
      Parameters:
      width - The width of the hitbox.
      height - The height of the hitbox.
      Returns:
      this sprite for chaining.

    • screenCenter

      public FlixelSprite screenCenter()
      Centers this sprite on the screen.
      Returns:
      this sprite for chaining.

    • screenCenter

      public FlixelSprite screenCenter(FlixelAxes axes)
      Centers this sprite on the screen.
      Parameters:
      axes - The axes to center on.
      Returns:
      this sprite for chaining.

    • destroy

      public void destroy()
      Description copied from class: FlixelBasic
      Cleans up this object so it can be garbage-collected. A destroyed FlixelBasic should not be used anymore. Use FlixelBasic.kill() if you only want to disable it temporarily and FlixelBasic.revive() it later.

      Override this function to clean up any resources used by this object, such as textures, fonts, sounds, etc.

      This function is called automatically when FlixelBasic.dispose() or FlixelBasic.reset() is executed, so you don't need to call it manually.

      Specified by:
      destroy in interface FlixelDestroyable
      Overrides:
      destroy in class FlixelObject
      See Also:
      • FlixelBasic.dispose()
      • FlixelBasic.reset()

    • hasOwnedGraphic

      public boolean hasOwnedGraphic()
      Whether this sprite holds an owned FlixelGraphic (e.g. from makeGraphic(int, int, Color)), so CPU-side pixmap uploads are allowed without mutating a shared atlas.
